Workout Warmup — R1

The pre-session warmup screen, re-clothed in Precision Blue. Frame 1 is the default collapsed state — protocol name rows with one-tap complete checks — carrying today's real Upper Body A warmup (Jump Rope, Abbreviated Ankle Protocol, Dynamic Warmup, straight from the program's warmup_protocols data). Frame 2 designs in #46: tapping a protocol's row discloses its real steps — sets, reps, hold times, and form cues — inline, without a modal. Judge: does expansion read as a quiet, optional detour rather than a gate, and is completing a protocol still one tap whether its card is open or closed.

1
Plantar Fascia Roll
30 sec/foot · Quick pass
2
Short Foot Exercise (Standing)
2×8 sec holds · Standing bilateral
3
Toe Yoga
1×8 reps · Quick set
4
Wall Ankle Mobilization (3-way)
8 reps each direction/side · Straight, inside, outside
5
Single-Leg Balance (Eyes Closed)
2×15 sec/side · Challenge proprioception
Dynamic Warmup
Pick 3–5 · 5 min
✓ ›
Continue to Exercises →
⌂Home
◉Workout
↗Progress
➜Run
One protocol expanded (#46)

Collapsed stays the default — a protocol name, an exercise count, and a duration are all most sessions need, and the moss ✓ is a single tap whether the card is open or closed. Tapping anywhere else on the row (never the check) discloses read-only reference detail per step — sets, reps, hold times, and a short form cue — pulled directly from the program's real warmup_protocols data; nothing shown here is invented copy. Steps carry no state of their own, so opening one can't half-finish a protocol: Frame 2 shows the Ankle protocol both done and expanded at once, proving the two are independent. Skip is deliberately the quietest control on the screen — plain text, no border, no fill, and it disappears once every protocol is touched, since there is nothing left to bypass. Continue stays quiet until all three protocols are touched, then arms (accent) — the same quiet-to-armed pattern as Finish on the logging screen.
